hypothesis: If universal machine-learning interatomic potentials trained on diverse datasets are used to model MOFs, then they will reproduce CP2K-PBE-D3(BJ) reference results across diverse MOF chemistries—yielding optimized geometries with volume deviations ≤5%, maintaining MD stability in 50 ps NpT simulations with volume changes ≤5%, predicting bulk moduli within 10% of DFT from Birch–Murnaghan fits, and computing 300 K heat capacities within 5% of phonon-derived DFT values—because the MLIPs capture the relevant interatomic interactions and transfer across porous frameworks.
